data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN (updated 2023); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; State Forest Portal

Q25.r: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN (updated 2023) explicitly mandates livelihood and income outputs: (a) Employment indicator — 5,200 guides/resort staff/drivers with local employment priority, licensed and tracked; (b) Community benefit indicator — 60% of tourism revenue to local communities; (c) Outcome Budget 2025-26 and CAG Report 2024-25 confirm these as mandatory output targets tracked in scheme MIS. The scheme's log-frame includes employment numbers, guide salaries, and community revenue share as primary output metrics. This satisfies condition (a) of T3.1 (mandatory output indicator measuring employment and income creation).
Q25.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Employment clause (5,200 local guides/staff), Community benefit clause (60% revenue); Outcome Budget 2025-26 — guides employed: 5,200, avg salary ₹3.2 lakhs; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — employment survey verified

observed.calc: Average annual salary per directly employed guide/resort staff/driver = ₹3.2 lakhs/year (₹3,20,000/HH/year). Source: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 verified from payment records for 5,200 registered guides/staff. This is per directly employed beneficiary household.
observed.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — average salary ₹3.2 lakhs calculated from payment records (5,200 guides confirmed); Outcome Budget 2025-26 — guides employed 5,200, avg ₹3.2 lakhs/year

Q26.r: Observed ΔY governs per framework rule. CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 independently verified average salary of ₹3.2 lakhs/year (₹3,20,000/HH/year) for 5,200 directly employed community members from payment records. This far exceeds the ₹3,000/HH/year observed threshold. No projection needed. For the 17,700 direct+indirect worker universe, applying 60% revenue share (₹111 cr across ~17,700 households) yields ~₹62,700/HH/year — also well above threshold.
docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — payment records, ₹3.2 lakhs avg salary verified
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — ₹3.2 lakhs salary, 5,200 direct + 12,500 indirect employment
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — community livelihood: 17,700 direct+indirect workers verified

Q27.r: Three distinct livelihood pathways are explicitly articulated in scheme documents: (1) Direct employment pathway — Official Notification mandates 5,200 guide/resort staff/driver positions with local employment priority; CAG and Outcome Budget track this as a primary output. Avg income ₹3.2 lakhs/year per person. (2) Community revenue-sharing pathway — Notification mandates 60% of annual tourism revenue (₹185 cr, so ~₹111 cr) distributed to local communities. This is a distinct income stream from employment — non-employed community members benefit. (3) Tribal cultural tourism pathway — Notification and Outcome Budget record heritage integration: 3 tribal groups showcased; tribal cultural tourism is an explicitly identified economic activity generating income for participating communities. Indirect supply-chain employment (12,500 workers per Economic Survey) is noted in evaluation data but not counted as a separate 'mandated' pathway since it is not independently articulated as a scheme design component in the notification.
Q27.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Employment clause (pathway 1), Community benefit 60% clause (pathway 2), Heritage integration clause (pathway 3); Outcome Budget 2025-26 — Heritage integration: tribal cultural tourism (3 tribal groups); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — employment survey, fund transfer records

Q28.r: An institutional offtake mechanism exists and is operational: (a) The Forest Department itself operates the ecotourism program as the implementing authority — tourism revenue collection (₹185 cr, entry fees ₹200–500/visitor) is through a government-managed mechanism with 185,000 annual visitors. (b) Guide licensing and employment contracts are managed by Forest Department + Tourism Department, providing an institutional employment channel for 5,200 community members. (c) The scheme provides a government-backed, demand-guaranteed channel: visitor targets (185,000/year) and eco-resort booking are managed via the state forest portal, providing institutional demand certainty for community guides and resort operators. This functions as government agency offtake (Forest Department/Tourism Department co-managed demand channel) — analogous to state-level government procurement scheme for community produce/services.
Q28.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Forest Department + Tourism Department implementation; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — ₹185 cr revenue confirmed via revenue ledgers, 185,000 visitors from entry records; State Forest Portal — resort booking, guide hiring as institutional channel

Q29.r: The scheme MIS and Outcome Budget track monetary/employment metrics only: visitor count (185,000), revenue (₹185 cr), guide count (5,200), salary (₹3.2 lakhs avg), employment count (17,700), satisfaction rating (85%). No non-monetary livelihood indicators for beneficiary households are tracked — no food sufficiency months, nutritional diversity metrics, fodder availability, or medicinal plant access indicators appear in Outcome Budget, CAG report, or portal statistics. Note: ΔY (₹3,20,000/HH/year observed) is far above the pass threshold (₹3,000), so the near-threshold flag is NOT triggered. Absence of T3.5 is noted as a governance gap but does not affect T3 pass/fail.
Q29.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 — metrics listed (all monetary/employment); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — audit scope (no non-monetary livelihood indicators audited); State Forest Portal — tracked statistics (all monetary)

pass_verification.enh_T3.5_flag: ok — ΔY (₹3,20,000/HH/yr observed) is far above threshold; near-threshold flag not triggered. T3.5=0 noted as governance enhancement gap.
